assumptions: Please act as a professional verilog designer.
Implement a Mealy FSM detection circuit that detects a single-bit input IN. When the input is 10011, output MATCH is 1, and MATCH is 0 in other cases. Support for continuous input and loop detection.
The module implements an FSM detection.
On every change in the input signal (IN) or positive edge of CLK or RST, if RST is active, the output signal MATCH is set to 0.

If the sequence of inputs IN is 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, the MATCH signal is 1 at the same time as the last occurrence of IN=1. If the sequence of inputs IN is 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, the MATCH signal becomes 1 at the fifth and ninth of IN; otherwise, it is set to 0.
Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

cases.goal: Confirm that the FSM does not assert MATCH for a single non‑matching input and that reset clears MATCH.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Conservative: only check that MATCH stays 0 after a single IN toggle that does not complete the pattern.

expected_properties: MATCH goes high exactly when the fifth input of pattern 10011 is observed

cases.goal: Validate that the Mealy FSM asserts MATCH on the correct input transition of the detection pattern.
notes: Uses conservative observation after each clock edge; does not assume exact latency beyond Mealy behavior.
preconditions: Reset deasserted and FSM in idle state.
stimulus_intent: Drive the input sequence 1,0,0,1,1 on IN while toggling CLK, then observe MATCH.
timing_assumptions: Observe MATCH on same clock edge as last '1' of the pattern (Mealy), but allow one-cycle observation window.
